claim: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is approximately 2x faster than previous Sonnet versions while maintaining better quality, addressing latency bottlenecks in agentic workflows
actionable: Upgrade to Sonnet 4.5 when latency is a constraint in agentic loops; the 2x speedup enables longer iteration cycles within same time budget

claim: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is significantly better at planning tasks and maintaining multi-hour agent sessions, with 18% improvement on agent reliability benchmarks
actionable: Use Sonnet 4.5 for long-running agent tasks that require planning and state management over hours of operation

claim: Claude Agent SDK includes context editing on the backend platform, allowing agents to summarize and remove earlier context while maintaining access to file-based memory
actionable: Leverage context editing to enable multi-hour agent runs by freeing up context window while maintaining file-based memory of decisions

claim: Sonnet 4.5 is aware of its context window limits and actively edits context during long runs, which requires rethinking agent prompts and verifiers to account for context manipulation
actionable: When using Sonnet 4.5 in long-running agents, test and verify that context editing doesn't break assumptions in your prompts or verifiers

claim: Claude Agent SDK emphasizes agentic search (agents actively searching for relevant context) over semantic search (RAG-style vector search) for agent workflows
actionable: Use agentic search for agent workflows instead of pure semantic search; combine with RAG when agents need tool-based retrieval

claim: Verification is a critical and underappreciated part of agent architecture - using MCPs, playwright, and LLM-as-judge to verify work enables reliable agent loops
actionable: Build custom verifiers for your agentic loops using MCPs (like playwright) to validate work and provide feedback for improvement
